description: Add BlockProber interface and sysfs-based implementation. Add interface to internal/iface/iface.go. Create implementation at internal/iface/osimpl/block.go that reads /dev/disk/by-label/<label> for FindByLabel and /sys/block/ for ListDisks. Wire into modes.Deps, finalize.Finalizer, and main.go. Replace all blkid/lsblk shell-outs in disk.go, live.go, grow.go with BlockProber calls. Update mode.Detector.BlockProber in main.go to use the new implementation. Add mocks and update all existing tests.
steps: Step 1: Add BlockProber interface to internal/iface/iface.go with FindByLabel(label string) (string, error) and ListDisks() ([]string, error) methods.

Step 2: Create internal/iface/osimpl/block.go (with //go:build linux) implementing BlockProber using os.Readlink on /dev/disk/by-label/<label> for FindByLabel, and os.ReadDir on /sys/block/ for ListDisks (filtering out loop/ram/dm devices).

Step 3: Create internal/iface/osimpl/block_test.go with unit tests for the osimpl implementation (testing path construction and error handling using filesystem abstractions or test-friendly patterns).
Step 4: Add BlockProber field (iface.BlockProber) to modes.Deps struct in internal/boot/modes/handler.go.
Step 5: Add BlockProber field (iface.BlockProber) to finalize.Finalizer struct in internal/boot/finalize/finalize.go.
Step 6: Add MockBlockProber to internal/boot/modes/mock_test.go with compile-time interface check.
Step 7: Add MockBlockProber to internal/boot/finalize/mock_test.go with compile-time interface check.
Step 8: Replace blkid -L K3OS_STATE shell-out in internal/boot/modes/disk.go SetupMounts() with h.deps.BlockProber.FindByLabel('K3OS_STATE').

Step 9: Replace blkid -L K3OS shell-out in internal/boot/modes/live.go SetupBase() with l.deps.BlockProber.FindByLabel('K3OS'). Replace lsblk -o NAME,TYPE -n shell-out with l.deps.BlockProber.ListDisks().
Step 10: Replace blkid -L K3OS_STATE shell-out in internal/boot/finalize/grow.go GrowLive() with f.BlockProber.FindByLabel('K3OS_STATE').
Step 11: Update internal/boot/modes/disk_test.go to use MockBlockProber instead of mocking Cmd.RunOutput('blkid', ...) calls.
Step 12: Update internal/boot/modes/live_test.go to use MockBlockProber instead of mocking Cmd.RunOutput('blkid'/'lsblk', ...) calls.
Step 13: Update internal/boot/finalize/finalize_test.go GrowLive tests to use MockBlockProber instead of mocking Cmd.RunOutput('blkid', ...) calls.

Step 14: Update main.go postChroot(): (a) Add BlockProber to modeDeps using osimpl.SysfsBlockProber{}. (b) Add BlockProber to fin (Finalizer). (c) Update the mode.Detector BlockProber func field to use the new osimpl implementation's FindByLabel method.
Step 15: Remove the standalone blockProbe helper function from main.go.
Step 16: Run go mod tidy to add u-root dependency (only if needed - the sysfs implementation may not need u-root at all since we read /dev/disk/by-label/ and /sys/block/ directly).
Step 17: Run golangci-lint run ./... and fix any issues. Run go test -race -covermode=atomic -failfast ./... and confirm all tests pass.
acceptance_criteria: No blkid or lsblk shell-outs remain in internal/boot/modes/ or internal/boot/finalize/ packages
BlockProber interface exists in internal/iface/iface.go with FindByLabel and ListDisks methods
A production implementation exists in internal/iface/osimpl/block.go
modes.Deps has a BlockProber field and all mode handlers use it
finalize.Finalizer has a BlockProber field and GrowLive uses it
mode.Detector.BlockProber in main.go uses the new osimpl implementation
The standalone blockProbe function is removed from main.go
All existing tests pass with updated mock expectations
findings: All tests pass. /sys/block and /dev/disk/by-label are not available in the sandbox environment so the osimpl unit tests skip gracefully. Shell tests needed updating too since ShellHandler uses LiveSetup which calls BlockProber. Review findings addressed in follow-up commit: added zram/nbd/sr/md to ListDisks filter, removed dead parseDisks/splitLines/splitFields helpers and their tests, simplified Detector closure to use method value, added label sanitization rejecting '/' and '..'.
